The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.

It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== Burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.

This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =[[File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg|240px]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}| coordinates={{coord|33|14|14.5|N|130|31|03.4|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}| 形状={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=Total length 107 m| 築造年代=Early to mid-5th century| 埋葬施設={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| 被葬者=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}| 出土品=Stone figures, haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13rd year }} (1938)| 特記事項={{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== Burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_other_designations of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>

On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>

The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.

'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== Burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =[[File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg|240px]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}| coordinates={{coord|33|14|14.5|N|130|31|03.4|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}| 形状={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=Total length 107 m| 築造年代=Early to mid-5th century| 埋葬施設={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| 被葬者=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}| 出土品=Stone figures, haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13rd year }} (1938)| 特記事項={{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== Burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== Burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =[[File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg|240px]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}| coordinates={{coord|33|14|14.5|N|130|31|03.4|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}| 形状={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=Total length 107 m| 築造年代=Early to mid-5th century| 埋葬施設={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| 被葬者=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}| 出土品=Stone figures, haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13rd year }} (1938)| 特記事項={{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].

The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.

It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>

The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "burial Facility" to "burial"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =[[File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg|240px]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}| coordinates={{coord|33|14|14.5|N|130|31|03.4|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}| 形状={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=Total length 107 m| 築造年代=Early to mid-5th century| 埋葬施設={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| 被葬者=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}| 出土品=Stone figures, haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13rd year }} (1938)| 特記事項={{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =[[File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg|240px]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}| coordinates={{coord|33|14|14.5|N|130|31|03.4|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}| 形状={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=Total length 107 m| 築造年代=Early to mid-5th century| 埋葬施設={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| 被葬者=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}| 出土品=Stone figures, haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13rd year }} (1938)| 特記事項={{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =[[File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg|240px]] |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}| coordinates={{coord|33|14|14.5|N|130|31|03.4|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}}| 形状={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| area=Total length 107 m| 築造年代=Early to mid-5th century| 埋葬施設={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| 被葬者=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}| 出土品=Stone figures, haniwa| 史跡指定=Designated as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13rd year }} (1938)| 特記事項={{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important Cultural Property of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been other_designation1 as a [[National other_designation of Japan]].

On March 24, 1978, additional other_designations and integrations expanded its status under the name {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}.<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing other_designations for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified other_designation.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} is placed. The lid features a {{ill|kirizuma|ja|寄棟造}} roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人}}, other_designation1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from {{ill|Ishigamiyama Kofun|ja|石神山古墳}} in {{ill|Miyama, Fukuoka|ja|みやま市}}, is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}, 8 minutes by taxi from {{ill|Nishimuta Station|ja|西牟田駅}}. * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}}, take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} towards {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}, disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of other_designations of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]
