{{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa|WD=Q1206740}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.23736111|N|130.51761111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=Total length 107 m| built=Early to mid-5th century| Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Interred Persons=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}| Excavated Artifacts=Stone figures, haniwa| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 13|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1938年|1=1938|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18645}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1938)| Special Notes=[[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]| architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional Historic Sites and integrations expanded its status under the name [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing Historic Sites for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkayadani Kofun|qq=|1=Kōkadani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990964}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified Historic Site.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. The lid features a [[:en:Hip roof|kirizuma]] roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone human figure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石人石馬|1=sekijin sekiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994947}}, Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from [[:en:Sekijinsan Kofun|Ishigamiyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama, Fukuoka]], is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kusazuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional Historic Sites and integrations expanded its status under the name [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing Historic Sites for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkayadani Kofun|1=Kōkadani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990964}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified Historic Site.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. The lid features a [[:en:Hip roof|kirizuma]] roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone human figure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石人石馬|1=sekijin sekiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994947}}, Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from [[:en:Sekijinsan Kofun|Ishigamiyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama, Fukuoka]], is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kusazuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].

{{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa|WD=Q1206740}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.23736111|N|130.51761111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=Total length 107 m| built=Early to mid-5th century| Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Interred Persons=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}| Excavated Artifacts=Stone figures, haniwa| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 13|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1938年|1=1938|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18645}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1938)| Special Notes=[[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]| architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional Historic Sites and integrations expanded its status under the name [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing Historic Sites for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkayadani Kofun|1=Kōkadani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990964}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified Historic Site.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. The lid features a [[:en:Hip roof|kirizuma]] roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone human figure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石人石馬|1=sekijin sekiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994947}}, Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from [[:en:Sekijinsan Kofun|Ishigamiyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama, Fukuoka]], is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kusazuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional Historic Sites and integrations expanded its status under the name [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing Historic Sites for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkayadani Kofun|1=Kōkadani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990964}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified Historic Site.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. The lid features a [[:en:Hip roof|kirizuma]] roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone human figure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石人石馬|1=sekijin sekiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994947}}, Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from [[:en:Sekijinsan Kofun|Ishigamiyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama, Fukuoka]], is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kusazuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa|WD=Q1206740}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.23736111|N|130.51761111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=Total length 107 m| built=Early to mid-5th century| Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Interred Persons=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}| Excavated Artifacts=Stone figures, haniwa| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 13|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1938年|1=1938|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18645}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1938)| Special Notes=[[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]| architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional Historic Sites and integrations expanded its status under the name [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing Historic Sites for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkayadani Kofun|1=Kōkadani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990964}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified Historic Site.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. The lid features a [[:en:Hip roof|kirizuma]] roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone human figure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石人石馬|1=sekijin sekiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994947}}, Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from [[:en:Sekijinsan Kofun|Ishigamiyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama, Fukuoka]], is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kusazuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

{{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa|WD=Q1206740}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.23736111|N|130.51761111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=Total length 107 m| built=Early to mid-5th century| Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Interred Persons=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}| Excavated Artifacts=Stone figures, haniwa| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 13|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1938年|1=1938|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18645}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1938)| Special Notes=[[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]| architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional Historic Sites and integrations expanded its status under the name [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing Historic Sites for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkayadani Kofun|1=Kōkadani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990964}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified Historic Site.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. The lid features a [[:en:Hip roof|kirizuma]] roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone human figure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石人石馬|1=sekijin sekiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994947}}, Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from [[:en:Sekijinsan Kofun|Ishigamiyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama, Fukuoka]], is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kusazuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].

{{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa|WD=Q1206740}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.23736111|N|130.51761111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=Total length 107 m| built=Early to mid-5th century| Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Interred Persons=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}| Excavated Artifacts=Stone figures, haniwa| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 13|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1938年|1=1938|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18645}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1938)| Special Notes=[[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]| architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional Historic Sites and integrations expanded its status under the name [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing Historic Sites for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkayadani Kofun|1=Kōkadani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990964}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified Historic Site.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. The lid features a [[:en:Hip roof|kirizuma]] roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone human figure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石人石馬|1=sekijin sekiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994947}}, Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from [[:en:Sekijinsan Kofun|Ishigamiyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama, Fukuoka]], is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kusazuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>

The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional Historic Sites and integrations expanded its status under the name [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing Historic Sites for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkayadani Kofun|1=Kōkadani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990964}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified Historic Site.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. The lid features a [[:en:Hip roof|kirizuma]] roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone human figure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石人石馬|1=sekijin sekiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994947}}, Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from [[:en:Sekijinsan Kofun|Ishigamiyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama, Fukuoka]], is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kusazuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses|石人山古墳 in Hirokawa, Fukuoka|the kofun in Miyama, Fukuoka|Sekijinzan Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=石人山古墳| image =File:Sekijinsan kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa|WD=Q1206740}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.23736111|N|130.51761111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| area=Total length 107 m| built=Early to mid-5th century| Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Interred Persons=(Presumed) Grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}| Excavated Artifacts=Stone figures, haniwa| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] in {{ill|Shōwa 13|ja|昭和13年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 13|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1938年|1=1938|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18645}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1938)| Special Notes=[[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]| architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Sekijinzan Kofun''' (石人山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
The tomb is believed to be the burial site of the grandfather of {{ill|Tsunokimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunokimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}. It was previously associated with Tsunokimi Iwai himself.<ref name=iwato>{{cite book|title=岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録|pages=14-16}}</ref>
== Overview == * Mound length: Approximately 107 meters<ref name=iwato /> * Front section width: Approximately 63 meters; height: Approximately 11 meters * Rear section diameter: Approximately 53 meters; height: Approximately 12 meters
The tumulus consists of two tiers in the front section and three tiers in the rear. A {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} structure remains in the northern constriction area. The mound originally had surrounding moats and embankments, remnants of which can still be observed on the west side of the front section and the east side of the rear section.
Based on its type and the dating of Sue pottery burial goods, the tumulus is believed to have been constructed in the early to mid-5th century. It is thought to predate {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, the burial site of Tsunokimi Iwai, by several generations, potentially making it the tomb of Iwai’s grandfather.<ref name=iwato />
The kofun was Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] on August 8, 1938. On March 24, 1978, additional Historic Sites and integrations expanded its status under the name [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite book|title=図説日本の史跡3 原始3|publisher=同朋舎出版|year=1991|pages=206}}</ref><ref>The pre-existing Historic Sites for {{ill|Iwatozan Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, Sekijinzan Kofun, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkayadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkayadani Kofun|1=Kōkadani Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17990964}} were integrated, and three additional kofun (Maruyamazuka Kofun, Maruyama Kofun, Chausuzuka Kofun) were included under the unified Historic Site.</ref>
=== burial Chamber === [[File:石人山古墳家型石棺.JPG|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] At the center of the rear section is an ancient {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with flat stones of green chlorite schist. Inside, a horizontal, assembled {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} is placed. The lid features a [[:en:Hip roof|kirizuma]] roof design, with a base length of 2.8 meters and a suspension groove on the gable for a stone sword. The body comprises four slabs, with area of 2.3 meters in length and 1.4 meters in height.
The lid is adorned with relief carvings of arc patterns and concentric circles, painted in cinnabar, making it a significant early example of decorated kofun.<ref name=iwato /> Although the stone chamber was opened during the Edo period and its ceiling is now missing,<ref>{{cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典|page=313}}</ref> it is protected by a facility that allows visitors to view it.
=== Stone Figure === [[File:石人山古墳石人.JPG|thumb|upright|Stone figure]] In front of the stone chamber, a {{ill|stone human figure|ja|石人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone human figure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石人石馬|1=sekijin sekiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17994947}}, Historic Site1 as an Important designation1 of Japan, is displayed in the {{ill|Sekijin Shrine|ja|石人社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This armed figure, which gave the kofun its name, stands with its back to the chamber. The figure, along with a similar one from [[:en:Sekijinsan Kofun|Ishigamiyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama, Fukuoka]], is among the oldest of its kind in the Southern Chikugo region.<ref name=iwato />
Carved from Aso welded tuff, the figure measures approximately 1.8 meters in height. It wears a triangular plate cuirass and {{ill|kusazuri|ja|草摺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kusazuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a groove on its left side indicates where a stone sword was once attached.<ref name=iwato />
The figure's face is now eroded, and its upper body patterns are unclear. Historical sketches from the Edo period depict its original appearance, showing eyes, a nose, and a mouth. It is believed that the figure became worn over time due to a folk practice of touching or striking it to cure physical ailments.<ref>[http://www.city.chikugo.lg.jp/kankou/_1070/_6135/_1088.html 石人山古墳の武装石人 - 筑後市]</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q498366}} [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]], 8 minutes by taxi from [[:en:Nishimuta Station|Nishimuta Station]]. * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]], take a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} towards [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]], disembark at "Ichijō," and walk 15 minutes (1.2 km). * 4.8 km from {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]].
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu-Okinawa)#Fukuoka Prefecture|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/kurashiguide/kyouikubunkasports/kyouiku_60.htm 広川町文化財 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=28&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Sekijinzan Kofun - Decorated Kofun Database (Kyushu National Museum)]
